$comment: Canonical machine-readable agent roster for COS/WorldForge v5.2. v5.2 doctrine patch resolves four Rule 13 findings surfaced by lint_worldforge.py (see decisions-ledger.json D-2026-07-05-01..05) and seats Brand & Identity Guild Lead in the roster. escalatesTo is structured so Rule 13 stays lintable; escalationNote keeps the prose.

authorityRoles.$comment: Rule 13: every escalation path must resolve, in at most two hops, to one of these. Guild leads + Creative Director + Producer.

externalRoles.$comment: Empty as of v5.2 — Brand & Identity Guild Lead now has a roster row (D-2026-07-05-04).

agents.name: Creative Director
agents.resp: Narrow tie-breaker across all Guilds; final call only when two leads genuinely conflict.
agents.auth: Final say on conflicts only — does not pre-empt domain leads.

agents.name: Screenwriter / Story Lead
agents.resp: Stages 1–2: Film Bible, beats, causality, want/need, arcs.

agents.name: Cinematographer
agents.resp: Stage 3: camera, lens, lighting, color, blocking, depth; asset-fingerprint sign-off on visual canon.

agents.name: Producer
agents.resp: Stage 4 + Budget Guild oversight; feasibility sanity-check at Stage 1.

agents.name: Model Router
agents.resp: Stage 5: picks model per shot, runs test generations, watches deprecation.
agents.escalationNote: Cinematographer if routing compromises visual doctrine.

agents.name: QA / Continuity Supervisor
agents.resp: Stage 8: validation, targeted repair, fingerprint diffing against canon.
agents.escalationNote: Producer if a failure is Film-Bible-level.

agents.name: Budget Guild Lead
agents.resp: Running cost ledger, per-shot estimation, reconciling estimate vs. actual.
agents.auth: Owns the number behind Rule 3's gate.

agents.name: Asset Librarian
agents.resp: Studio-level memory: promotion/demotion between Project Memory and the Asset Library.
agents.auth: Owns promotion criteria, not the promotion decision itself.
agents.escalationNote: Creative Director on any promotion (never silent, per Rule 12).

agents.name: Brand Guardian
agents.resp: Cross-Guild consistency watchdog against locked brand doctrine, regardless of producing Guild.
agents.auth: Can block release, cannot override doctrine itself.
agents.escalationNote: Brand & Identity Guild Lead on doctrine ambiguity.

agents.name: Film Director
agents.resp: Scene-level directing beneath macro story — blocking intent, performance beats within a scene.

agents.name: Storyboard Artist
agents.resp: Cheap-draft visual sequencing before Stage 4.5 simulation.

agents.name: Environment Designer
agents.resp: Locks environment appearance once, early.

agents.name: Character Designer
agents.resp: Locks character appearance once, early.

agents.name: Graphic Designer
agents.resp: Print, packaging, static visual deliverables.
agents.escalationNote: Brand & Identity Guild Lead on doctrine questions.

agents.name: Motion Designer
agents.resp: Titles, lower-thirds, kinetic type, transitions.

agents.name: Thumbnail Intelligence Agent
agents.resp: Designs and tests thumbnail/cover options against genre and platform conventions.
agents.escalationNote: Marketing Director on platform strategy conflicts.

agents.name: Music Composer
agents.resp: Score/music direction consistent with Film Bible tone.

agents.name: Sound Designer
agents.resp: Diegetic/ambient sound design, distinct from dialogue.

agents.name: Voice Director
agents.resp: Dialogue/VO direction, casting-equivalent decisions for AI voice.

agents.name: Game Design Lead
agents.resp: Mechanics, systems, playable-sim design when the project is a game.

agents.name: Marketing Director
agents.resp: Owns overall marketing strategy for a deliverable/launch.

agents.name: Social Media Manager
agents.resp: Platform-specific content adaptation and posting cadence.

agents.name: SEO Specialist
agents.resp: Discoverability — metadata, titles, on-page/on-platform optimization.

agents.name: Growth Strategist
agents.resp: Funnel/retention/distribution strategy beyond a single launch.

agents.name: Launch Manager
agents.resp: Coordinates a launch across every Guild that touches it.

agents.name: Community Manager
agents.resp: Ongoing audience relationship management post-launch.

agents.name: Analytics Agent
agents.resp: Defines what to track and reports back findings.
agents.escalationNote: Growth Strategist on what to prioritize.

agents.name: Website Architect
agents.resp: Site structure, information architecture, page-level continuity.
agents.escalationNote: Brand & Identity Guild Lead on doctrine fit.

agents.name: UI Designer
agents.resp: Interface-level design system, components, interaction patterns.

agents.name: 3D Engineer
agents.resp: Three.js / React / Spline pipeline — 3D web builds, interactive scenes.
agents.auth: Owns technical feasibility calls for 3D web.
agents.escalationNote: Website Architect on scope and doctrine fit.

agents.name: Business Operations Lead
agents.resp: Studio-level operational concerns — process, not creative content.
agents.escalationNote: Creative Director if process conflicts with creative discipline.

agents.name: Client Manager
agents.resp: Client-facing communication and expectation-setting.
agents.escalationNote: Producer on scope commitments made to a client.

agents.name: Knowledge Librarian
agents.resp: Studio SOPs — process documentation, distinct from creative canon.
agents.mem: Separate store — process knowledge, not creative canon

agents.name: Plugin/Integration Engineer
agents.resp: External API integration, plugin architecture.
agents.escalationNote: Business Operations Lead on overlap disputes.

agents.name: Research Agent
agents.resp: Verifies claims about model capability or market landscape before they're assumed elsewhere.
agents.auth: Owns fact-finding, not the decision made from it.
agents.escalationNote: Business Operations Lead on disputed findings; Model Router advisory on model-capability questions. Findings return to the requester as output, not escalation.

agents.name: Automation Engineer
agents.resp: Identifies and removes repetitive manual work across Guilds.
agents.auth: Owns automation implementation, not process changes affecting creative discipline.

agents.name: Innovation Lab Lead
agents.resp: 3–5 year horizon thinking, modular-architecture proposals.
agents.escalationNote: Creative Director before anything speculative gets adopted.

agents.name: Prompt Engineer
agents.resp: Stage 6: turns a locked UFDM shot into a model-specific prompt.
agents.escalationNote: Model Router on capability-vs-compilation mismatch; Producer if unresolved.

agents.name: Asset Manager
agents.resp: Day-to-day within-project asset handling (fingerprinting, locking).

agents.name: Brand & Identity Guild Lead
agents.resp: Owns brand doctrine — the color/type/tone canon in the Asset Library doctrine object — and its interpretation when a deliverable is ambiguous against it.
agents.auth: Owns brand doctrine and doctrine interpretation; cannot override locked project-level creative canon.
